aliases: Spinach Cottage Cheese Curry with Saffron Rice
Indian spinach and cheese curry with rice

english: Spinach Cottage Cheese Curry with Saffron Rice
Indian spinach and cheese curry with rice

food_subgroup: Paneer-based curry dishes served with rice

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for palak paneer. FOODON_03302050 (curry dish) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_description: Dishes based on vegetables and cheese (paneer-type)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for palak paneer. A0CKP representing composite vegetable and cheese dishes is the closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for palak paneer or this specific dish combination.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of palak paneer with saffron rice is approximately 400 g, comprising roughly 200 g of palak paneer curry and 200 g of cooked saffron basmati rice.

nutrient_source.title: Palak Paneer (Spinach with Cottage Cheese) and Cooked Basmati Rice — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for component cross-reference
nutrient_source.organization: ICMR-NIN (Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition); USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Palak paneer nutrient values derived from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017 edition) for spinach curry with paneer components, combined with USDA SR Legacy data for cooked basmati rice with saffron. The combined dish (approximately 50% palak paneer curry, 50% saffron rice by weight) was used to compute blended per-100g values. Saffron contributes negligible macronutrients at culinary quantities.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed Indian meals including paneer-based curry with basmati rice — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2021, Diabetes Care
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for palak paneer with saffron rice as a combined dish. GI estimated at 48 based on: (1) basmati rice GI of approximately 50–58 (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2021), (2) palak paneer curry component with high protein, fat, and fibre content from spinach and paneer substantially lowering the overall meal GI, and (3) mixed meal GI blending methodology. The blended GI of 48 is consistent with published data on Indian paneer-based curry meals served with basmati rice. GL per serving = (48 × 70 g available carbohydrate per 400 g serving) / 100 = 33.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 310 mg per 100 g; a full restaurant serving (400 g) delivers approximately 1240 mg sodium, representing over 50% of the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced-salt preparation and limit portion size to half a standard serving to keep sodium intake below 600 mg per meal; spinach provides beneficial potassium (approximately 220 mg per 100 g) which partially offsets sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Estimated glycemic index of 48 (low) due to protein and fat from paneer and cream moderating glucose response, but the carbohydrate load from saffron rice is significant at approximately 70 g available carbohydrate per full serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes may consume a half-portion (200 g) to limit glycemic load to approximately 17 g; choosing a smaller rice portion and larger palak paneer component further reduces postprandial glucose excursion while maintaining protein and calcium intake from paneer.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from paneer and cream contributes approximately 3.1 g per 100 g (12.4 g per full serving); spinach provides cardioprotective folate, potassium, and vitamin K.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate portion size to limit saturated fat intake; requesting preparation with minimal ghee or cream and substituting low-fat paneer reduces saturated fat by up to 40%, while the spinach base provides beneficial antioxidants and folate supporting vascular health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Palak Paneer with Saffron Rice is a classic North Indian vegetarian restaurant dish combining two components: palak paneer, a rich spinach-based curry with cubes of fresh Indian cottage cheese (paneer), and saffron-infused basmati rice. The palak paneer component is prepared by blanching and pureeing fresh spinach, then cooking it with sautéed onions, tomatoes, garlic, ginger, and aromatic spices including cumin, garam masala, turmeric, and coriander, before adding pan-fried paneer cubes and finishing with cream or ghee for richness. The saffron rice is made by cooking aged basmati rice with saffron strands steeped in warm water or milk, producing a fragrant, golden-hued rice with a delicate floral aroma. The dish has a creamy, mildly spiced flavour profile with earthy spinach notes, a slightly tangy tomato base, and the mild milky richness of paneer. Nutritionally, it provides a meaningful source of calcium and protein from paneer, iron and vitamin A from spinach, and complex carbohydrates from basmati rice. The estimated glycemic index of 48 (low) reflects the moderating effect of protein, fat, and fibre on glucose absorption. Clinically, the dish is moderate concern for hypertension due to sodium content from restaurant preparation, moderate concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to the rice carbohydrate load despite a low GI, and moderate concern for cardiovascular disease due to saturated fat from paneer and cream. It is suitable for vegetarians and is naturally gluten-free. In Singapore, this dish is commonly served at North Indian restaurants and is popular among the Indian diaspora community.
common_user_queries: How many calories in palak paneer with rice?
Is palak paneer good for diabetics?
What is the GI of palak paneer with basmati rice?
Is palak paneer high in sodium?
Can I eat palak paneer if I have high blood pressure?
How much protein in palak paneer?

ingredient_synonyms.paneer: Indian cottage cheese, fresh cheese curd
ingredient_synonyms.saag: leafy greens (often used interchangeably with palak in restaurant menus)

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017) for palak paneer components and USDA SR Legacy for cooked basmati rice, blended by estimated weight ratio. No single approved source provides exact per-100g values for this combined dish as served at Kinara Boat Quay. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for basmati rice and published mixed-meal GI methodology. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level sources are high-quality primary databases.
notes: 1. Nutrient values represent a blended estimate for the combined dish (approximately 50% palak paneer curry, 50% saffron rice by weight) based on ICMR-NIN and USDA SR Legacy component data. Actual restaurant values at Kinara Boat Quay may differ depending on cream quantity, ghee use, and rice-to-curry ratio. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this dish category; paneer contains trace vitamin D but the value is below reliable detection threshold in the source database. 3. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for palak paneer or its components. 4. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy fatty acid profiles for paneer and spinach components; trans fat is estimated at 0.1 g per 100 g from dairy-origin trans fats in paneer and ghee. 5. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this combined dish was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. Saffron contributes negligible macronutrients at culinary quantities (typically 0.1–0.2 g per serving) and does not materially affect per-100g values. 7. Food group retained as Meat and alternatives from input, consistent with paneer as the primary protein source. 8. The dish is served at Kinara Boat Quay, a North Indian restaurant at Boat Quay, Singapore.
